Records.description: One-loop equal-mass banana (bubble) integral in $D=2-2\varepsilon$ dimensions. Trivial case: the change of variables from $y$ to $\bar{q}$ rationalises the square root $\sqrt{1+4y}$. Two master integrals $I_{10}$ and $I_{11}$. Discussed as the $l=1$ degenerate case of the all-loop formula.

Equal-mass one-loop two-point function $I_{
u_1 
u_2}(D,x)$ with $q_1=k-p$, $q_2=k$, $m_1=m_2=m$, $x=-p^2/m^2$. Used as worked IBP and differential-equation example: IBP reduces all integer $(
u_1,
u_2)$ to two masters $\{I_{11}, I_{10}\}$; pre-canonical 2x2 DE matrix in eq. example_A_oneloop_twopoint and eps-form transformation in eq. trafo_bubble produce uniform-weight expansions in 4D and 2D (eqs. example_bubble_uniform_weight_4D/2D).
Records.location: Chapter 'Iterated integrals', Eq. (chapter_iterated_integrals:example_equal_mass_bubble, line ~113); Example 1 of Sec. 'Differential equations' line ~922

Records.description: 1-loop equal-mass bubble illustrating eps-factorized DE via coordinate change rationalizing $\sqrt{-x(4-x)}$.
One-loop bubble with equal internal masses. Used to demonstrate expansion by regions in large-mass and small-mass limits with pySecDec.

One-loop massive bubble (self-energy) integral with two equal-mass internal propagators and off-shell external momentum $p$. Used as the introductory example to demonstrate positivity constraints and semidefinite programming. The master integrals $I_{2,1}$ and $I_{3,0}$ (UV-finite basis) are evaluated numerically to $\sim 10^{-14}$ relative accuracy at $p^2=2$, $m^2=1$ using positivity constraints in both loop-momentum and Feynman-parameter space. Epsilon expansion terms computed to $\mathcal{O}(\varepsilon^2)$ via both direct positivity constraints and numerical differentiation w.r.t. spacetime dimension.
Records.location: Eq. (2.1), Section 2, Fig. 1; analytic result Appendix A Eq. (A.3); numerical results Eqs. (2.43), (2.73)-(2.74), (2.78)-(2.79)

Records.description: One-loop equal-mass bubble used as benchmark family for IBP reduction via reinforcement learning and simulated annealing. Numerical kinematics $p^2=1$, $m^2=2$, $D=5/7$. No analytic evaluation.

One-loop two-point (bubble) family $I(a_1,a_2)$ with two equal-mass propagators $D_1=k^2-m^2$, $D_2=(k+p)^2-m^2$ and $p^2=s$, defined in eq. (3.1). IBP reduction yields two master integrals: tadpole $I(1,0)$ and finite bubble $I(2,1)$. The paper derives the all-order epsilon expansion via differential equations, expressing the result through HPLs in $x=(\sqrt{1-4m^2/s}-1)/(\sqrt{1-4m^2/s}+1)$. Section 3.2 gives the exact hypergeometric representation $I(2,1) = -(1/2) \Gamma(1+\varepsilon) (m^2)^{-\varepsilon-1} {}_2F_1(1,1+\varepsilon;3/2;s/(4m^2))$.

Records.authors: Gonzalez, Ivan, Kondrashuk, Igor, Moll, Victor H., Recabarren, Luis M.
Records.description: One-loop bubble $J(\alpha,\beta,m,m)$ with two equal-mass propagators of generic powers in $D$ dimensions. Mellin-Barnes representation converted to bracket series via method of brackets, yielding four 3F2 hypergeometric terms.
Records.familyName: Equal-mass one-loop 2-point bubble with general propagator powers (2 propagators)

Records.description: One-loop massive bubble with two massive internal propagators (mass $m_2$) and two massive external legs (mass $m_1$). The Baikov polynomial $b(z)$ is quadratic in $z_1, z_2$. The diagonal basis has 3 master integrals across all sectors. Used as a benchmark for the diagonal basis prescription and closed formula for intersection numbers.

Records.authors: Charalampos Anastasiou, Stefan Beerli, Stefan Bucherer, Alejandro Daleo, Zoltan Kunszt
Records.description: The authors give the two-loop master integrals for the production of a Higgs boson via a massive quark and a squark loop.

Banana family, banana_L1_equal-mass-internal. Banana (sunrise) graph: two vertices joined by L+1 edges, L loops, equal internal masses. Evaluates to Calabi-Yau (L-1)-fold periods; GKZ systems give the all-L structure; eps-factorized at all L.

quadruple: {E^(eps*EulerGamma)*Gamma[eps], (x1 + x2)^(2*(-1 + eps))/(-(mm*x1^2) - 2*mm*x1*x2 + MM*x1*x2 - mm*x2^2)^eps, {x1, x2}, {mm, MM}}
